assertions.description: The design has 8 P-MOSFETs (Q1-Q8: 4x DMG2305UX + 4x BCM857BS dual PNP), 8 resistors (R1-R8, 100k), 2 connectors (J1, J2, 1x05), 2 fiducials (FID1, FID4), 3 others (G1 SparkFun logo on-board, G2 SparkFun logo off-board, G3 OSHW logo). Total = 23, which matches statistics.total_components=23.

statistics.power_rails correctly lists GND, VIN1, VIN2, VIN3, VIN4, VOUT1, VOUT2, VOUT3, VOUT4. These correspond to the 4 independent ideal diode circuits each with a separate input and output power rail, all sharing GND. The enumeration is accurate.

signal_analysis.transistor_circuits correctly identifies all 8 transistors: 4x DMG2305UX P-MOSFET (Q2, Q4, Q6, Q8) with gate_pulldown resistors R2, R4, R6, R8 respectively, and 4x BCM857BS dual-PNP BJT (Q1, Q3, Q5, Q7) with base resistors R1, R3, R5, R7. The is_pchannel=true for the MOSFETs is correct for DMG2305UX (P-channel). The BJT base_net and collector_net both resolving to unnamed nets is expected since the base-collector feedback loop creates unnamed internal nets.

INCORRECT: The analyzer classifies Q1, Q3, Q5, Q7 (BCM857BS dual PNP) with emitter_net pointing to VIN1/VIN2/VIN3/VIN4 respectively. The BCM857BS is a dual PNP transistor acting as a comparator/controller driving the P-MOSFET gate. In the ideal diode circuit, the BJT collector drives the MOSFET gate; the emitter connects to VIN. The analyzer's emitter assignment to VIN is consistent with the schematic nets, but the collector_net and base_net are both resolving to the same unnamed internal net (__unnamed_X), which suggests the dual transistor's internal feedback connection (base of transistor A connected to collector of transistor B for the cross-coupled control) is not properly decomposed. This causes the 'collector_is_power=false' and 'load_type=resistive' which are somewhat misleading for this gate-drive application.

INCORRECT: The component_types shows 2 fiducials (FID1, FID4) and 3 others (G1, G2, G3). However the BOM lists only FID4 and FID1 as fiducials, which is correct — there are indeed 2 fiducials. G1, G2, G3 are logos (3 others). But G3 is the OSHW_Logo with in_bom=false and is excluded from the statistics count, while G1 and G2 are SparkFun logos with in_bom values of true and true/false respectively. The 3 'other' count includes G1 (on_board=false, in_bom=true), G2 (on_board=false, in_bom=true), and G3 (on_board=true, in_bom=false). This count is technically correct given the in_bom/on_board flags, but may be confusing since G3 is not in the BOM.

The design implements 4 identical ideal diode circuits, each consisting of a P-MOSFET (DMG2305UX, Q2/Q4/Q6/Q8) controlled by a dual PNP transistor pair (BCM857BS, Q1/Q3/Q5/Q7) with a 100k gate drive resistor. This is a classic ideal diode / ORing controller circuit using discrete components. signal_analysis.protection_devices is empty, missing 4 instances of this protection topology. The transistor_circuits array correctly identifies the 8 transistors but classifies them as generic mosfet/bjt switches rather than as components of an ideal diode protection circuit.
